title: CC (or BCC) multiple users or Guests on an Outlook Email
excerpt: As of this writing, Monday.com Outlook integration supports sending separate emails to multiple people, however it does not support ccing or bccing such that users can send a single email to multiple people. I’ve seen so…

posts.author: zorlove (Zachary)
posts.cookedHtml: As of this writing, Monday.com Outlook integration supports sending separate emails to multiple people, however it does not support ccing or bccing such that users can send a single email to multiple people. I’ve seen some discussion on this topic in the past and have seen that solutions enabling users to send separate emails to multiple people have been deemed a viable solution to the problem of not being able to cc or bcc on the Outlook integration. Sending multiple emails is not a viable solution, it creates multiple email threads on the same topic which leads to a mismatch of information across the various recipients and makes for an awkward business workflow. Am I missing a workaround that will enable me to cc or bcc, or is Monday still working to implement this feature?

posts.author: Mondae (Andy Ehle)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been a workaround for this. I want to be able to send an outlook email to more than one person, either as the direct recipient or a cc. I dont want to use the text workaround because someone has to manually input that for every single item. Any ideas?

posts.author: MrMonday2023 (Mr Monday)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this? Or the post that has the latest updates on this issue\feature?

posts.author: ahogan (Abbigail Hogan)
posts.cookedHtml: I just learned you can do this by using a text column instead of an email column, and seperating the emails with a space ; space (person@email.com ; person2@email.com)

posts.author: bouchboud (Nicolas Bouchard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, but is there another way to send the same email to 2 different persons without being in the same text column?

My problem is that 1 name in that column will change on every project, but the other name is a fix one. Also, I’m using that column for a another automatic email sending that I do not need the fix email on it.

In a nutshell, it would be awesome if we can just CC a fix email in the Automatisation request.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: This solution still sends multiple mails each with its own “thread”. No CC/BCC.

posts.author: michaeljamesromt (michaeljamesromt)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re right that sending separate emails instead of being able to cc or bcc creates extra threads and confusion, especially when everyone needs to stay aligned on one topic. At the moment there isn’t really a built-in workaround that fully solves this in the Outlook integration. Some users have tried creating shared mailboxes or group addresses as a partial fix, but it’s not the same as true cc or bcc support. Hopefully Monday adds this functionality soon since it would make workflows much smoother and communication more professional.

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
If I may suggest a potential workaround. You can try using SuperMail, it’s a native monday.com marketplace app that allows sending automated designed emails, cc and BCC as well. From Outlook or Google as well.

SuperMail – Email Automation - monday.com Marketplace SuperMail – Email Automation - monday.com Marketplace
